articles.title: How China’s Weapons Transfers to Iran Have Evolved Over Decades
articles.summary: China has denied claims this month by U.S. intelligence that it might have shipped arms to Iran. In recent years, China has supported Iran with dual-use parts.

articles.title: Trump’s Blockade Risks Upending an Emerging Détente With China
articles.summary: In a thinly veiled critique of the war in Iran, China’s leader said the world could not risk reverting “to the law of the jungle.”

articles.title: Ship ‘Spoofing’ in Strait of Hormuz May Compound Confusion
articles.summary: A new pattern of deceptive activity by some vessels around the critical waterway suggests the new American blockade is changing how some ships linked to Iran are behaving.

articles.title: Trump administration moves to erase Jan. 6 riot convictions for seditious conspiracy
articles.summary: The Trump administration is moving to vacate the seditious conspiracy convictions of extremists involved in the Jan. 6, 2021 attack, who earlier received commutations instead of full pardons.

articles.title: Trump Increased Tax Refunds, but a Political Challenge Still Looms
articles.summary: While last year’s tax law has raised the average I.R.S. refund, the boost is less than the White House expected — and may not have much impact on voters.

articles.title: Trump says new talks with Iran will happen soon. And, Eric Swalwell faces new allegations
articles.summary: President Trump says new talks with Iran could happen in the next two days. And, Democrat Eric Swalwell faces new allegations as a second woman comes forward accusing him of rape.

articles.title: Trump says peace talks could resume in days as U.S. military blocks Iranian ports
articles.summary: President Trump said a second round of direct U.S.-Iran peace talks could resume in Pakistan within the next two days, even as he instituted a naval blockade of all Iranian ports.

articles.title: Fela Kuti is the first African artist to enter the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
articles.summary: In December, the late Nigerian superstar became the first African musician to get a Grammy lifetime achievement award. Now he's making history as well at the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame.

articles.title: The Republicans Who Disapprove of the War, and a Wave of College Closures
articles.summary: Plus, will the World Cup bring tourists back to the U.S.?

articles.title: Tax season was supposed to bring big refunds. So far they're less than expected
articles.summary: The average refund so far is $350 more than last year at this time, despite projections that it would be closer to $1,000 due to Republican-led tax changes as part of the Big Beautiful Bill Act.

articles.title: Pope heads to Cameroon as separatists announce 3-day pause in fighting
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV is heading to the central African nation of Cameroon with a message of peace for its separatist region and for talks with President Paul Biya.

articles.title: No one likes being discombobulated. How did the feeling get such a fun name?
articles.summary: Sometimes you just need to recombobulate. That word isn't in the dictionary, but it is on a beloved sign at Milwaukee's airport.

articles.title: 'No peace': Nearly a year after her son's death, she learned that ICE was responsible
articles.summary: Ruben Ray Martinez is considered the first person to be killed by ICE during President Trump's second term. His mother believes his death could have been avoided.

articles.title: Vance Says Pope Leo Should Be More Careful When Talking About Theology
articles.summary: The vice president, who is Catholic, took issue with Pope Leo XIV’s statement that disciples of Christ are “never on the side of those who once wielded the sword and today drop bombs.”

articles.title: 'Humiliated, broken, powerless': Sudan enters fourth year of war
articles.summary: While parts of Sudan's capital show fragile signs of life, across the country the conflict between the army and a rival paramilitary continues to drive mass displacement, hunger and allegations of atr

articles.title: As fuel prices continue to rise, Iran war drives Europe toward new crisis
articles.summary: As fuel protests bring Ireland to a standstill and UK leaders decry price hikes driven by the Iran war, Europe -- barely recovered from the energy shock of the Ukraine war -- faces another crisis.

articles.title: IMF predicts Iran war will slow economic growth and raise inflation globally
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund expects the Iran war to slow the global economy and raise inflation. NPR's Michel Martin discusses the forecast with Simon Johnson, a former IMF chief economist.

articles.title: Trump slams Italian prime minister, one of his few remaining European allies
articles.summary: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Italian journalist Viviana Mazza about President Trump's criticism of Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni, one of few remaining allies in Europe.

articles.title: Prosecutors Make Surprise Visit to Fed as Pirro Defends Investigation
articles.summary: The Justice Department’s criminal inquiry into the Federal Reserve threatens to delay the confirmation of the next chair.

articles.title: King Charles III and Queen Camilla Will Visit D.C., New York and Virginia
articles.summary: The planned four-day state visit comes at a fraught time in the U.S.-U.K. relationship, following President Trump’s frequent belittling of the British prime minister.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
